label: strict-cellwise-frontier
headline_statement: Refuted-with-counterexample: the stated \(K=2,n=4\) witness law \(P^\star\) has \(\mathcal F_2(P^\star)=\varnothing\), so the advertised finite compatibility gap \(\Gamma_2(u;P^\star)\) is not a well-defined sparse-versus-cellwise support-program comparison.
witness_construction_if_refuted: For the proposed P^star, units 2 and 3 have four distinct observed neighbor-response means at a=0: unit 2 has 0.33, 0.44, 0.40, 0.51 and unit 3 has 0.35, 0.46, 0.42, 0.53. If either incident edge is absent, observed means vary with an omitted neighbor; if both incident edges are present, any K=2 exposure map must merge two distinct means. Thus every one of the eight candidate hidden-edge subsets violates K-support consistency and observed-law matching, so F_2(P^star) is empty.
